sanskrit:
रजसि प्रलयं गत्वा कर्मसङ्गिषु जायते।
तथा प्रलीनस्तमसि मूढयोनिषु जायते॥
transliteration:
rajasi pralayaṃ gatvā karma-saṅgiṣu jāyate;
tathā pralīnas tamasi mūḍha-yoniṣu jāyate.
literal_meaning: Having attained dissolution in rajas, one is born among those attached to action; similarly, dissolved in tamas, one is born in the wombs of deluded species.
contextual_meaning: The mind that dies gripped by restless craving is reborn into a life of endless hustle; the mind that dies numbed or spiteful sinks into a life of instinctual blindness. Both are self-chosen traumas recycled until insight dawns.
psychological_state: Hyper-aroused productivity or hypo-aroused shutdown become post-mortem developmental pathways; the verse warns that the final affective tone before sleep/death crystallizes into next-morning/ next-life identity.
trigger_scenario: After a 90-hour work-month you collapse into a Netflix-and-junk-food coma; waking up you feel more bovine than human—this micro-cycle previews the macro-cycle described in the verse.
anchor_text: Your last thought seeds your next life—choose clarity over craving or apathy.
